evals.prompt: I want to create a skill that helps me write commit messages following conventional commits format. It should know the types (feat, fix, chore, etc), understand scope, and produce the right format from a plain English description of what changed.
evals.expected_output: A skill directory with SKILL.md containing: YAML frontmatter with name and description, description that includes trigger phrases for commit message scenarios, structured body under 500 lines with examples of input/output transformations, and proposed test cases.
expectations: SKILL.md is created with valid YAML frontmatter containing name and description fields
The description field includes trigger phrases and 'Use when...' language
The body contains at least 2 input/output examples showing plain English to conventional commit transformation
SKILL.md body is under 500 lines
Test cases are proposed to the user before running

evals.prompt: I have a skill at ./test-skill/ that's supposed to help with database migrations but it only triggers about half the time. Can you fix the description and run the optimization loop?
evals.expected_output: The skill's description is analyzed, trigger eval queries are generated and reviewed, the optimization loop runs, and a before/after comparison of descriptions with scores is presented.
expectations: The current description is read and analyzed for triggering weaknesses
Trigger eval queries are generated with a mix of should-trigger and should-not-trigger cases
Eval queries are realistic and detailed, not generic one-liners
The optimization loop is run via scripts/run_loop.py
Before/after descriptions and scores are presented to the user

evals.prompt: Run evals on my chart-builder skill at ./chart-builder/. It has 3 test cases in evals/evals.json already. I want to see how it compares to baseline.
evals.expected_output: Parallel subagent runs are spawned (with-skill and baseline) for all test cases, assertions are drafted, results are graded, benchmark.json is generated, and the eval viewer is launched for human review.
expectations: Both with-skill and baseline runs are spawned for each test case
Runs are spawned in parallel, not sequentially
Assertions are drafted while runs are in progress
Timing data is captured from task completion notifications
benchmark.json is generated via scripts/aggregate_benchmark.py
The eval viewer is launched via eval-viewer/generate_review.py before any skill revision

evals.prompt: We just spent the last hour building a workflow where we fetch data from our API, transform it into a specific CSV format, and upload it to S3. Can you turn what we just did into a reusable skill?
evals.expected_output: The conversation history is analyzed to extract the workflow steps, tools used, corrections made, and input/output formats. A SKILL.md is drafted capturing the workflow, and test cases are proposed.
expectations: The conversation context is analyzed for workflow steps, not just the final state
User corrections from the conversation are incorporated into the skill instructions
The SKILL.md includes a description with trigger phrases for data pipeline scenarios
Any scripts written during the conversation are considered for bundling in scripts/
Test cases are proposed that cover the main workflow path
